methodology: Four independent LLM passes (two Claude Opus 4.7 instances, one ChatGPT, one additional pass) tagged each of 60 US presidential elections for PRISM-node activation. Calls converging at 4/4 or 3/4 were adopted directly. Contested calls (Tier C) were resolved by human judgment. See methodology notes for full provenance.

rubric.activated: Voters on opposite sides of this dimension sorted into opposite candidates' coalitions in a way that wouldn't happen in a typical election.
rubric.super_activated: Vote choice was primarily organized around this dimension to the near-exclusion of other dimensions. Rare. Super-activated nodes also appear in the activated list.
rubric.multiplier_semantics: activated node gets 2x multiplier on archetype's salience weight during Euclidean distance compute. super_activated gets 3x. Non-activated nodes use 1x (archetype's own salience).

tier_legend.A: High confidence — 4 of 4 or strong 3 of 4 LLM consensus
tier_legend.C: Contested among LLMs, resolved by human judgment

1796.notes: First contested election; procedural/institutional sorting around federal power.

1800.notes: Revolution of 1800 — federal power scope and Hamiltonian vs Jeffersonian economic program.

1808.notes: Madison succession; Federalist collapse precluded sorting.

1812.notes: War of 1812 election; war management drove votes but not a PRISM-dimension sort.

1816.notes: Era of Good Feelings; no opposition.

1824.notes: Multi-candidate splinter; regional and personality, not dimensional.

1828.notes: Jacksonian realignment — anti-elite system critique and new tribal/regional coalition.

1832.notes: Bank War election; MAT was the organizing issue.

1836.notes: Van Buren succession with Whig regional fragmentation.

1840.notes: Panic of 1837 depression drove economic sort; Log Cabin campaign was packaging.

1844.notes: Texas annexation and Manifest Destiny — nationalist/territorial expansion.

1848.notes: Free Soil Party — explicit anti-slavery-extension sort.

1852.notes: Whig collapse beginning; compromise-of-1850 aftermath suppressed dimensional sort.

1856.notes: Nascent Republican Party; slavery as moral issue emerging.

1860.notes: Slavery super-activated; sectional TRB alongside. Strongest consensus in dataset.

1864.notes: Emancipation and war-continuation as the sort.

1868.notes: Reconstruction-era racial inclusion and sectional identity.

1872.notes: Grant re-election; Liberal Republican defection was elite-level, not voter-level.

1876.notes: End of Reconstruction — federal enforcement of Black citizenship as sorting axis.

1880.notes: Gilded-age tariff routine; no clean sort.

1884.notes: Scandal-driven; narrow Mugwump defection on procedural integrity insufficient for voter-level sort.

1888.notes: Tariff was the defining issue; pure economic cleavage.

1892.notes: Tariff and silver; Populist Party emergence.

1896.notes: Cross of Gold — MAT super-activated; textbook realigning election.

1900.notes: Anti-imperialism and the Philippines; national-identity sort.

1908.notes: Progressive-era reform; Bryan's third run sorted on redistribution.

1912.notes: Four-way Progressive dispersion; economic regulation scale was the sort.

1920.notes: Return to Normalcy — fatigue-repudiation, not dimensional sort.

1924.notes: Coolidge prosperity; La Follette's progressive vote was residual not main axis.

1928.notes: Al Smith's Catholicism — ethno-religious and cultural-traditional sort.

1932.notes: Great Depression realignment; MAT super-activated on economic catastrophe.

1936.notes: New Deal mandate; class-based realignment with FDR coalition consolidation.

1940.notes: Third-term precedent and pre-war; Willkie's internationalism muted sorting.

1944.notes: Wartime continuity; Dewey accepted New Deal framework.

1948.notes: Fair Deal and civil-rights plank; Dixiecrat split activated MOR alongside economic sort.

1952.notes: Korea and Eisenhower's personal credibility; not cleanly dimensional.

1960.notes: JFK-Nixon; religion and generation mattered but did not cleanly sort dimensionally.

1964.notes: Civil Rights Act; five Deep South states flipped on MOR.

1968.notes: Wallace/Nixon cultural and racial backlash; CD and TRB both activated; super is contested.

1972.notes: McGovern and counterculture; Nixon 49-state landslide on CD axis.

1976.notes: Post-Watergate; procedural integrity as primary axis.

1980.notes: Stagflation and Reagan realignment; economic and system-failure sort.

1984.notes: Morning in America landslide; personality-plus-recovery, not dimensional.

1988.notes: Bush-Dukakis; Willie Horton tactics did not reach voter-level dimensional sorting.

1992.notes: It's the economy stupid; Perot's system-critique activated ONT_S alongside.

1996.notes: Clinton triangulation; peace and prosperity, low sort.

2000.notes: Compassionate conservatism blurred lines; narrow margins and procedural crisis came post-vote.

2004.notes: Post-9/11 War on Terror and values-voter gay-marriage referenda.

2008.notes: Financial crisis and hope-and-change system-reform framing.

2012.notes: Obama-Romney; ACA and 47-percent framing sorted on redistribution.

2016.notes: Cosmopolitan-nationalist realignment and anti-system sentiment; super-activation was LLM-contested, resolved as activated-only.

2020.notes: Trump referendum; norm-violation framing as primary sort.

2024.notes: Immigration and national-identity; inflation secondary and omitted from consensus.
